config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama karena kalau kepentingan সমাজ—eh, masyarakat—dijadikan prioritas mutlak, itu mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak berdaya. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan hak dasar seperti kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati, bukan dikalahkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to excuse abuse of minorities and unpopular people. At the same time, individual rights are not a blank check to ignore public safety, public health, or basic civic obligations, so there are real limits. What matters to me is a strong presumption in favor of individual rights, with society only overriding them when there is a clear, narrow, and well-justified need. If we flip that presumption, it becomes too easy for the majority to silence dissent and call it the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman melihat politik dan birokrasi di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, mengabaikan prosedur, atau membenarkan tindakan sewenang-wenang. Hak individu memang bukan alasan untuk merusak ketertiban umum, tetapi justru harus menjadi batas agar mayoritas tidak mudah menindas minoritas atas nama kebaikan bersama. Yang lebih sehat adalah menempatkan kepentingan publik dan hak individu dalam keseimbangan, dengan hak dasar tetap punya perlindungan paling kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general rule, because in the United States "society first" has too often been the excuse for trampling dissent, minorities, and unpopular speech. Public safety and public health matter, but they should justify limits on individual rights only in narrow, evidence-based ways, not as a default moral hierarchy. The problem with putting society first is that "society" usually means whoever has power at the moment, and I do not trust that to protect people who are vulnerable or outnumbered. For me, a free society is one where individual rights are the starting point, and collective needs have to earn their exceptions.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutup penyalahgunaan wewenang dan membungkam kelompok kecil. Bagi saya, kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak dasar individu, terutama hak atas kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum. Kalau hak individu dijadikan nomor dua, yang paling sering menang justru bukan masyarakat, melainkan pihak yang paling kuat atau paling berkuasa. Jadi, yang lebih adil adalah menempatkan kepentingan bersama dalam batas yang jelas, dengan hak individu tetap menjadi pagar utamanya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society ahead of the individual as a general principle, because in the United States that phrase has repeatedly been used to justify overreach, censorship, and discrimination against minorities. The argument that public good matters is real, but it does not erase the fact that rights are exactly what protect people when the majority gets it wrong or when leaders abuse power. I am fine with limited, evidence-based restrictions for things like public health or safety, but those should be exceptions that have to be justified, not the default rule. To me, a healthy society is one that earns legitimacy by respecting individual rights, not by treating them as secondary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
